item.title: Eiffel Tower, Iron Lady
item.description: A wrought iron structure of unparalleled posterior breadth, famed for its steadfast refusal to be anything but thiccc; often admired astern against a tumultuous sky.

item.title: Cactus, Saguaro
item.description: A towering succulent native to the Sonoran Desert, renowned for its expansive girth and impressive stature; often observed with reverence from a safe distance.

item.title: Column, Doric Greek
item.description: A structural pillar of disciplined stoutness, characterized by its fluted shaft and austere capital, often observed in ancient temples where its presence remains unchallenged.

item.title: Sequoia, General Sherman
item.description: A giant sequoia tree renowned for its extraordinary mass and height, particularly when viewed from ground level where the sky becomes a mere afterthought.

item.title: Boulder, Glacial Erratic
item.description: A substantial rock formation, often resting incongruously upon a landscape, relocated by ancient glacial activity, notable for its commanding presence when viewed astern in natural light.

item.title: Tabular Iceberg, Antarctic
item.description: A colossal slab of ice, characterized by its flat top and steep sides, often mistaken for a floating geological feature. Its mass and breadth defy casual navigation, esp. when viewed in stark contrast against the horizon.

item.title: Spruce, Suburban
item.description: A mature evergreen specimen (genus Picea) cultivated as a residential or municipal landscape feature; characteristically pyramidal, with branches sweeping low to the ground in a dense conical mass.
